text.title: nodejs-express Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for nodejs-express as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Placing errorHandler in the middle of the middleware stack or not registering it at all; async errors from routes go uncaught.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: errorHandler must be the final middleware. Express 4 does not auto-forward async errors (Express 5 does). Routes MUST call next(error) on catch or use an asyncHandler wrapper.

failure-mode.description: Putting business logic in controllers or routes; mixing res.status/.json patterns with custom error classes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Controllers bloat. Routes become unreadable. Custom error handling becomes inconsistent across endpoints. Use services for logic, one error handler for responses.

failure-mode.description: Validating only req.body and ignoring req.params (e.g., /users/:id where id is not validated) or query parameters.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unvalidated params/query lead to silent failures. parseInt(req.params.id) can fail. Use validateAll with schemas for all three.

failure-mode.description: Throwing generic Error or returning res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal error' }) directly from routes; inconsistent error shapes across endpoints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generic errors lose context. Route-level error handling is repeated. One handler is auditable and provides observability.

failure-mode.description: Using multiple type definitions for req.user across different files or allowing routes to mutate req.user without type safety.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Type drift. One source of truth (types/express.d.ts) ensures consistency. Mutations are visible if type is readonly.

failure-mode.description: Not handling SIGTERM; calling process.exit() immediately; not draining DB/queue connections on shutdown.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Container orchestrators will kill the process forcefully. Connection leaks prevent clean rollouts. Infra restarts multiple times per deployment.
